title: cyperus ferax var. auriculatus
intro: Cyperus ferax var. auriculatus, also known as the 'eared papyrus', is a variety of the Cyperus ferax plant, a type of sedge that is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cyperus ferax var. auriculatus are attributed to its ability to promote digestion, relieve menstrual cramps, and lower blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, and it can also be dried and used in potpourri or as a natural remedy for insect bites.
